lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Ah, look what we have here, Will! This looks like someone who needs a good job!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Goodness, Anne, I think you're right! This one's clearly never worked an honest day in their life, and it's about time someone changed that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't need a...

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Alas! We are sadly not currently in a place where we can take on any more staff, but let us hope that changes soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Well, let's not waste any more time! Stranger, are you ready for your interview?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess so...?

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Let's get you started with a nice easy question: What is your name?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [player name].

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Pleased to meet you, [player name]. I'm Anne, and this is Will.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Next question: Do you have any experience captaining a ship?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really...

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Will, it's important we not be too picky! It's a competitive market, after all.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Okay, final question: Do you agree to waive all rights to pursue legal action against your new employer in the event of injury or horrific death?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that likely to happen?

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Not at all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure if I...

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Excellent! Well then, [player name], I'm pleased to say that we have the results of your interview. You bring some impressive stuff to the table. We'd love to offer you the role!

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks... only, you didn't actually mention what the role was...

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Ah, how forgetful of us! Given that you've already accepted, how about we discuss the details on our ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I didn't accept anything...

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Just let us know when you're ready, and we'll hop aboard!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'm ready...

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Oh dear! It doesn't quite look like you are! You'll need to make some room to carry some things before we head off!

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Then let us away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather not...

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Well, don't take too long. This work won't do itself, will it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I'm not looking for a job right now. I'm just going to go...

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Oh, you'll be back, friend! You already know you can't turn down an opportunity like this!

sections.title: Talking to Anne or Will after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Ah, there you are! Time to get on with your interview!

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Let's get you started with a nice easy question: What is your name?

sections.title: Talking to Anne or Will after being offered the job

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Ah, there you are, [player name]. Are you ready to hop aboard our ship?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess so...

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Then let us away!

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Lovely! The open sea!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not really that open... We haven't even left port yet.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Well, good news, [player name], because that's where you come in! You're our new helm!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Helm?

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: It means you're in charge of the ship's helm! In other words, you're responsible for steering the ship!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's more of a boat really...

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Well, we all have to start somewhere!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... So if I'm manning the helm, what will you two be doing?

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Well, it's simple, isn't it? We're in charge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you ever actually done this before?

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Many times!

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Just never on our own ship before! This is an exciting first day for... well, we haven't actually named our business yet, but I'm sure we'll come up with a good name soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what does this business do?

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: We salvage shipwrecks of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. Alright then, I don't remember doing it, but apparently I committed to this. Where is our first wreck?

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: We've got a good one to get us started! Last time we were on the Pandemonium, we purchased a map that should lead us to an old pirate wreck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Pandemonium?

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Have you never been? It's a great place for a drink! We'll have to take you there!

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: But first, let's collect some salvage! According to the map, the wreck is just east of the Karamja Shipyard, so south of here.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Over to you, [player name]! Take the helm when ready.

sections.title: Talking to Will or Anne before sailing off

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: This is your bit, [player name]! Take the helm when ready.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Remember, you need to take us to the wreck. It should be east of the Karamja Shipyard, so south of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I want to go back to Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: This isn't the can-do attitude we need, [player name], but fine, we'll go back...

sections.title: Deploying the salvaging hook before sailing off

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: You need to take us to the wreck before we can use the salvaging hook, [player name]. It should be east of the Karamja Shipyard, so south of here.

sections.title: Talking to Will or Anne back at Port Sarim

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Hello there, [player name]. Shall we hop back aboard our ship?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Oh dear! It doesn't quite look like you are! You'll need to make some room to carry some things before we head off!

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Excellent! Let us away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Well, don't take too long. This work won't do itself, will it?

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: The wreck should be south of here.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: The wreck should be south west of here.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: The wreck should be south east of here.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: The wreck should be north of here.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: The wreck should be north west of here.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: The wreck should be north east of here.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: You need to head south of here.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: You need to head south.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: You need to head north.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: You need to head west.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: You need to head east.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: We need to be going south.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: We need to be going north.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: We need to be going east.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: We need to be going west.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: You need to head south west.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: You need to head south east.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: You need to head north west.

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: You need to head north east.

sections.title: When the ship is losing wind

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: You need to trim the sails, [player name]! We need to catch that wind!

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: You need to trim the sails, [player name]! We need to catch that wind!

sections.title: If the player fails to gain more wind

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: [player name]! The sails need trimming! Don't miss out on that wind!

sections.title: If the wind is picking up

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: The wind's picked up, [player name]! You'll need to trim the sails!

sections.title: If the player deploys the salvaging hook but not at the salvaging location

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: You need to take us to the wreck before we can use the salvaging hook, [player name]. It should be east of the Karamja Shipyard, so south of here.

sections.title: If the wind picks up and the player trims the sails

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Very good!

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Good work!

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Nicely done!

sections.title: If the player sets the sails before starting to move the ship
Talking to Anne or Will after arriving at the salvaging point

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Right, now that we're here, I think it's time to do some salvaging!

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: When you're ready, [player name], just lower the salvaging hook and let's see what we can find!

sections.title: Talking to Anne or Will after they explain salvaging, or interacting with the helm or sails

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Time for some salvaging, [player name]! When you're ready, just lower the salvaging hook and let's see what we can find!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right then, let's give this a go.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you make of these?

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: The medallion! Anne, we've found it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You were looking for this?

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: That's right! And now that we've found it, I'm afraid we don't really need you anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Sorry, [player name]. If it's any consolation, you did a great job! Unfortunately, we just have to let you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eugh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where am I?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yo ho ho and a Jolly Roger to you, me hearty! I can see from the fact that you're totally drenched from head to toe that you are a fellow scallywag sailor! The name's Steve Beanie! Pleased to meet you.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Right, now that we're here, I think it's time to do some salvaging!

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: When you're ready, [player name], just lower the salvaging hook and let's see what we can find!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... why am I lowering the hook? I thought the helm was my responsibility?

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Yes! The helm... and the hook!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. I'll just do everything while you two reap the rewards. Sounds about right.

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Don't go getting grumpy on us now, [player name]. This isn't about the rewards. It's all about the experience!

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: That's right! Think of what you'll learn today!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh*

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yo ho ho and a Jolly Roger to you, me hearty! I can see from the fact that you're totally drenched from head to toe that you are a fellow scallywag sailor! The name's Steve Beanie! Pleased to meet you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I must be having some sort of fever dream... or maybe I'm dead?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Ha! Far from it, matey! Though the fever dream I can't comment on. They're rather common around here... but I assure you that it's well-nigh certainly like enough most likely not the rum doing it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... You said words there, but I don't think they actually formed a sentence.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Ah, me wee hearty! You seem to be having quite the crisis! Thankfully, you've rigged the starboard and docked yourself at the right port! Welcome to the Pandemonium!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pandemonium?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye! To be sure! This here is the finest establishment in all the seas. Sit back! Have a drink! Let the good times, and the rum, flow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If this is the Pandemonium, do you know of a pair of salvagers called Will and Anne?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Avast ye! You name a pair of pinching looting plundering seadogs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So you do know them. They claimed to have purchased a map here, but I'm guessing they weren't being totally honest about that...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Purchased?! Purchased?! You're pulling my leg, you scallywag! Those scurvy dogs came here and broke the one rule of the Pandemonium! That map was pilfered! Stolen!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I had quite the fun run in with them myself. They had me help them salvage an old shipwreck before throwing me overboard.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Arrr, you poor scallywag.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. It was the map they stole that led them to the wreck. What do you know about it?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Not a lot, I'm afraid. You should hoist your sails and go speak to Ribs instead. It was his map they looted. He's the old dog over in the corner. Avast!

sections.title: Talking to Squaking' Steve Beanie again

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: The scallywag sailor returns! Have you come for a drink? We only have the best here on the Pandemonium!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If this is the Pandemonium, do you know of a pair of salvagers called Will and Anne?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Avast ye! You name a pair of pinching looting plundering seadogs!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's see what you have.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Those plundering seadogs! Did you speak to Ribs about them? It was his map they looted. He's the old dog over in the corner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I'll go and speak to him now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I might have lost a cup here.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye! Someone handed it in earlier. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Blistering barnacles! You don't seem to have room for it. You'd better come back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yo ho ho!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, thanks.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Shiver me timbers!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Hello there. I'm afraid I only talk to proper sailors, and I can see you're not quite at that level yet. However, you are welcome to admire me from afar.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Can I help you, mate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Okay... You should probably get going then.

lines.speaker: Crew Registrar
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Crew Registrar
lines.text: I'm the crew registrar. Here to cut through the red tape of crew management for anyone who needs it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A lot of paperwork in managing a crew is there?

lines.speaker: Crew Registrar
lines.text: You'd be surprised. Quality assurance guideline QA-275 states all crew must be appropriately fit and healthy after being subjected to QA form QA-275.

lines.speaker: Crew Registrar
lines.text: QA-123 requires all crew to pass a mandatory cargo data protection course if the vessel can be fitted with a cargo hold.

lines.speaker: Crew Registrar
lines.text: Then of course we have QA-441, which defines the background checks that any crewmate operating a sail must pass. And all that's without even considering QA- 215...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That all sounds a bit much!

lines.speaker: Crew Registrar
lines.text: Well, it's not for everyone. Still, if you ever find yourself in need of a crew, you know where to find me.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: Ahoy, young whippersnapper. Have you come to hear a tale from old Ribs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kind of, I guess. I was hoping you could tell me about Will and Anne, along with the map they stole.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: A dark day. A very dark day. Those young fools know not what they have wrought upon themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That being?

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: A curse! A curse most foul! That map marked the resting place of the Crimson Kraken! She was a deadly vessel, captained by none other than Barnacle Barnaby himself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Barnacle Barnaby? Who's that then?

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: You have not heard the tale of Barnacle Barnaby? You have not heard of the scourge of the Unquiet Ocean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, tell me about him.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: He was a cruel and vicious pirate. Orchestrator of the Driftwood Massacre and unleasher of the Plague of Black Mists. He believed himself untouchable, but his arrogance was his undoing.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: Despite the warnings of many, Barnacle Barnaby attempted to plunder the Skullhorde, but his only reward was a deadly curse. It destroyed the Crimson Kraken, condemning all on board to a watery grave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you had a map leading to the wreck of the Kraken?

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: Aye! I purchased the map from Ulrich the Unfortunate, the sole survivor of the Kraken, but I had no intention of using it. I knew that no good would ever come from seeking the Kraken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then why not destroy the map?

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: And risk the wrath of Barnaby's ghost? No. Better the map be locked away, so that neither it nor the curse could harm anyone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure I quite follow your reasoning for keeping it, but I guess it doesn't matter now. I'm afraid Will and Anne found and looted the Kraken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now's not really the time for tales.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: You are a fool for thinking that way, but on your head be it. The short of it is that Barnaby and the Kraken were cursed, condemning him and his crew to a watery grave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well, I'm afraid Will and Anne found and looted the Kraken.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: I feared as much, though part of me still hoped it would not come to be. Tell me, did they find Barnaby's medallion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They did. It was actually the only thing they were interested in.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: Then the foul curse is now free once more. They must be stopped!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I do have my own personal reasons for wanting Will and Anne dealt with. Maybe I could try and find them.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: You are a brave soul indeed, but that is just what is needed at a time like this.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: You should talk to Steve. He already put out a bounty for Will and Anne after the theft. He might have heard something about where they could have gone.

lines.speaker: Ribs
lines.text: Ahoy, young whippersnapper. Did you speak to Steve about that bounty?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll go and see him now.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Will and Anne...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Those plundering seadogs! Did you speak to Ribs about them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did. He seems to think they might have unleashed some sort of curse.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Ah, the curse of Barnacle Barnaby! It's one of Ribs' favourite tales. Just a myth of course, but the old dog does love his stories.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, myths aside, Ribs mentioned you'd put a bounty out for Will and Anne. I'd be quite keen to track them down myself.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yo ho ho! A mighty good decision! It's a generous bounty indeed. You don't go breaking the one rule of the Pandemonium and expect to get away with it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly is this one rule you keep mentioning?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Avast! The mainland kingdoms have no authority here. Their laws do not apply. Instead, we just have our one rule that keeps the Pandemonium afloat: Never disrespect your fellow patron.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: We don't care who you are. Indeed, pirates and customs officers can enjoy a drink here, side by side. Savvy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So anyone is welcome here as long as they don't commit an offense against any of the other patrons?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! You've got it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So, back to Will and Anne. Do you have any idea where they might have gone?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Shiver me timbers! I'm afraid not. Old Grog said he might try and track them down. Perhaps he has an idea of where to find them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea where Will and Anne might have gone?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Shiver me timbers! I'm afraid not. Old Grog said he might try and track them down. Perhaps he has an idea of where to find them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And where would I find Old Grog?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Ah, the old scallywag left not that long ago, but he'll be back here before long. He enjoys a drink far too much! In the meantime, you should hoist the sails and ready your vessel!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have a vessel...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: What? But then how did you get here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will and Anne threw me overboard and I washed up here. I did mention this earlier...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Blistering barnacles! Well, you won't be getting far without a boat of your own. You should head to the docks and speak with Junior Jim, our local shipwright. He'll get you sorted out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll go and see him.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Jolly Roger!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Will and Anne...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye! Old Grog hasn't returned yet, but I'm sure he'll be back soon. In the meantime, you should head to the docks and speak with Junior Jim, our local shipwright. He'll get you sorted out with a boat of your own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll go and see him.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Jolly Roger!

sections.title: Talking to Junior Jim again before showing him the cup or using the cup on him

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: You're back! Do you have that cup? If so, let's have a gander at it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have lost it.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: You've got no boat. You've got no cup. You're fresh out of luck it would seem, mate. You'd best hope you lost it here on the Pandemonium. If so, someone might have handed it in to Steve.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I guess I'd better go see if Steve has it.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Hmm... Yeah, yeah, I can work with this. This old rusty cup will get you the finest raft I have!

sections.title: Talking to Junior Jim after giving him the cup

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Right, time to sort you out with a cargo hold. Let's head on over to the shipyard...

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Can I help you, mate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Junior Jim?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: That's me. What do you need then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Steve said you might be able to sort me out with a boat.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: You don't have a boat?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Ha! We've got ourselves a Billy-no-boats! Classic! Yeah, I can sort you out with a boat, but it's gonna cost you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Well, what do you have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I do have this cup I found. Perhaps I could trade it for a boat.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Alright, let's have a gander at it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have lost it.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: You've got no boat. You've got no cup. You're fresh out of luck it would seem, mate. You'd best hope you lost it here on the Pandemonium. If so, someone might have handed it in to Steve.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I guess I'd better go see if Steve has it.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Hmm... Yeah, yeah, I can work with this. This old rusty cup will get you the finest raft I have!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I think I must have misheard you. Did you say raft?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: That's right! The best one I have!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was kind of hoping for a bit more than a raft. It's not just the cup I have. I'm sure I can make up the funds to buy something bigger.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Sorry, mate, but even if you could, the raft is all I've got to hand right now. Besides, you're gonna want to build up some experience before you captain anything too big. A raft will be perfect for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Lighten up, mate. I'll have plenty more vessels in stock soon. Come back once you've got a bit more know-how and I'll be able to sort you right out.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Now, about your raft... With that cup I'll be able to sort you out with the finest helm, but it won't be enough to cover the sails.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I won't get anywhere without sails!

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Don't panic, you silly plonker. I ain't about to send you off on a raft with no sails. I'll sort them out for you, but I'll just need a little favour in return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What favour?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: I've got some cargo I need picking up from Port Sarim and bringing back here. In return for sorting you out with some sails, I'll need you to go and collect it for me.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Oh, and I tell you what, I'll even set you up with the materials to whack together a cargo hold. You won't be shifting that cargo far without one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Now, hand over that cup and we can get cracking.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Right, time to sort you out with a cargo hold. Let's head on over to the shipyard...

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: You'll want to grab yourself some tools from the crates over there, mate. After that, just use the gangplank to hop onto your raft. Once you're on board, you can get to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: You'll want to grab yourself some tools from the crates over there, mate. After that, just use the gangplank to hop onto your raft. Once you're on board, you can get to work.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Just use the gangplank over there to hop onto your raft. Once you're on board, you can use your tools to build a cargo hold. Don't worry about the materials, mate. I'm supplying those this time.

sections.title: Taking from the crate of hammers
Taking from the crate of saws
Modify Boat Schematics before building the cargo hold

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Very nice! Now, use the gangplank to hop back up here and we can get going.

sections.title: Attempting to modify the cargo hold

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Smashing job on that cargo hold, mate. Let's get back to the Pandemonium, shall we?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Okay, looks like that's you all sorted, mate. I'd say you're now ready to set sail!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How exciting!

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Why don't you take your new raft out for a spin? You can head over to Port Sarim and collect that cargo for me. Oh, which reminds me, now that you're officially a captain, you'll need one of these.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Ah, seems you don't have room for it, mate. Why don't you come back later?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Your very own captain's log! You can use that to keep track of your adventures out at sea, including any work you might end up taking on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like collecting your cargo?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Right on, mate! In fact, I'll make a note of that in your log now.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: And that's you all ready to go, mate! Just hop on your new raft when ready. If you need help finding my cargo, just check in with the port master when you get to Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Now, unless you fancy a cheeky dip, the gangplank down the docks will be your best bet for getting on board.

sections.title: Talking to Junior Jim after he gives you the Captain's log

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: You'll be needing one of these, mate.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Ah, seems you don't have room for it. Why don't you come back later?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: You should be all ready to go, mate! Just hop on your new raft when ready. If you need help finding my cargo, just check in with the port master when you get to Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Now, unless you fancy a cheeky dip, the gangplank down the docks will be your best bet for getting on board.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Port master
lines.text: Hi there. Do you need help with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to pick up some cargo for Junior Jim on the Pandemonium.

lines.speaker: Port master
lines.text: Looks like you already have it. You'll need to take it back to the Pandemonium now.

lines.speaker: Port master
lines.text: Ah, you must be new to this. You can pick up and drop off cargo just over there at the ledger table.

sections.title: Taking cargo from the ledger table
Depositing cargo at the ledger table
Disembarking the boat while the cargo is still in the hold
Talking to Junior Jim after he asks for the cargo

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Alright, mate. How are you doing collecting my cargo?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Well, you'd better hop back onto your raft then. Unless you fancy a cheeky dip, the gangplank down the docks will be your best bet for getting on board.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got it right here.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Very nice! Just hand that over to the port master and he'll sort it out. Come let me know once you're done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Port master
lines.text: Hi there. Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to drop off some cargo for Junior Jim.

lines.speaker: Port master
lines.text: You don't seem to have any cargo on you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I must have left it in my cargo hold. I'll be right back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to drop off some cargo for Junior Jim.

lines.speaker: Port master
lines.text: Ah, very good. Just put it over by the ledger table and I'll get it sorted.

sections.title: Dropping off the cargo on the ledger table

lines.speaker: Port master
lines.text: Thanks, sailor. I'll get that all sorted out. You should go let Jim know his cargo is here.

sections.title: Talking to the Port master after dropping off the cargo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Port master
lines.text: Hi there. Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fine, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Junior Jim after depositing the cargo

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Alright, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I collected your cargo and left it with the port master.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Very nice! That's us all squared up then. You ought to pop back once you've got a bit more experience with that raft. By then I should have some new vessels for sale.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might just do that. Any tips on the best ways to get that experience?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: To be honest, mate, I'd say one of the best things would be to take on more work like what you just did for me. A lot of ports will have a notice board where folks advertise jobs they want sorting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. Anything else?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Well, now that you're officially a big cheese captain, you might want to start charting the sea for your own records. That log book I gave you has all sorts of cracking tips on how to do so.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Oh, and while we're on about charting, I've also chucked some useful bits and bobs in your cargo hold.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: First up, I've left you a couple of repair kits in there. Perfect for if you go being a numpty and end up sailing straight into a proper big storm or a big group of sharks.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: The other thing in there is a spyglass. It's a proper handy charting tool that's great for getting a good look at things while out at sea.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: There's a chap called Charles who used to be pretty good at the whole charting thing who might be able to help you more. You can find him down in the cave on the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Thanks, Jim. I'd better go check in with Steve now and see if Old Grog is back.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Don't mention it, mate.

sections.title: Talking to Steve again after Old Grog departs

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yo ho ho, [player name]! Best you not waste time! Belay your helm and abaft your rigging! Get out there and make sure you're ready for Old Grog!

lines.speaker: Old Grog
lines.text: Is this the one?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye! This Jolly Roger here is pretty keen to see some justice dealt! Old Grog, meet... er...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [player name].

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: [player name]! And [player name], meet Old Grog.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleased to meet you.

lines.speaker: Old Grog
lines.text: Steve tells me you're keen to see Will and Anne dealt with. I'm open to sharing the bounty, but only if you have what it takes. I have no time for layabouts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't let you down!

lines.speaker: Old Grog
lines.text: We'll see. I saw that raft you rocked up in. If that's all you bring to the table, then you have some work to do.

lines.speaker: Old Grog
lines.text: It will take me some time to track Will and Anne down, but make no mistake, I will find them. I expect you to be ready by the time that happens. Don't be a disappointment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have my word.

lines.speaker: Old Grog
lines.text: Good. Now, that's me done drinking. Time to get to work. I'll keep Steve updated on my progress, so make sure you're checking in with him. He'll let you know once I have something.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yo ho ho, [player name]! Best you not waste time! Belay your helm and abaft your rigging! Get out there and make sure you're ready for Old Grog!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: To help you on your way, I've got some pirate looty here for you! Sawmill coupons! Useful if you want a top vessel like mine.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Oh, and don't forget to pop back for a drink from time to time. My rum is to die for! Now, avast!

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Ah, look what we have here, Will! This looks like someone who needs a good job!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Goodness, Anne, I think you're right! This one's clearly never worked an honest day in their life, and it's about time someone changed that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't need a...

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Alas! We are sadly not currently in a place where we can take on any more staff, but let us hope that changes soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Ah, look what we have here, Will! This looks like someone who needs a good job!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Will
lines.text: Goodness, Anne, I think you're right! This one's clearly never worked an honest day in their life, and it's about time someone changed that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't need a...

lines.speaker: Anne
lines.text: Alas! We are sadly not currently in a place where we can take on any more staff, but let us hope that changes soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...
